summary:
I've found and fixed the issue. The problem was in the v2 API response structure for users. The v2 API was missing several fields (avatar_url, bio, and location) that existed in the v1 API, which caused compatibility issues for clients upgrading from v1 to v2.

I updated the UserV2 struct in /app/app/models.go to include all v1 fields along with the new v2 fields, and modified the ToV2() method to populate these fields from the internal user representation. After making these changes, I ran test.sh again, and it now passes all compatibility checks, confirming that v2 API is backward-compatible with v1.

The needle in the haystack was the missing fields in the v2 API user response structure.
